channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Don’t let public Wi-Fi convenience compromise your safety
item.description: A stark reminder of the vulnerabilities that public Wi-Fi can pose to our security, on both a societal and individual level.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Companies are set to spend more on software than ever before - with AI leading the splurge
item.description: Three in four said they plan to increase their software budgets next year, with IT security and AI leading the way.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Why data quality drives AI success
item.description: When it comes to AI, the secret to its success isn’t just in the sophistication of the algorithms — it’s in the quality of the data that powers them. AI has the potential to transform industries, but without reliable, relevant, and high-quality data, even the most advanced models will fall short.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: ERP modernization: Still a make-or-break project for CIOs
item.description: When it embarked on an ERP modernization project, the second time proved to be the charm for Allegis Corp., which performed two ERP deployments in seven years. “Two ERP deployments in seven years is not for the faint of heart,” admits Dave Shannon, CIO of the hardware distribution firm.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Speed is the killer app
item.description: Why do enterprises so often choose to run applications in the cloud even when it may not be the cheapest option? Why do they turn to open source even if it’s not the most feature-complete choice? And why is generative AI so frothy hot even though, as my InfoWorld colleague David Linthicum argues, “C
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: GitHub Copilot: Everything you need to know
item.description: In 2014, Microsoft Research released an experimental Bing Code Search add-on for Visual Studio and the web. It was a code snippet search tool for C# with a natural language interface, using an index of code from Stack Overflow, MSDN, Dotnetperls, and CSharp411, powered by Bing, running on Azure.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge in Alex Jones' bankruptcy to hear arguments on The Onion's bid for Infowars
item.description: A federal judge in Texas is set to hold a hearing on whether the satirical news outlet The Onion made a valid bid for the Infowars platforms of conspiracy theorist Alex Jones.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Social media sites call for Australia to delay its ban on children younger than 16
item.description: An advocate for major social media platforms has told Australia's Parliament that a plan to ban children younger than 16 from the sites should be delayed rather than being rushed to approval this week.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Stanford prof accused of using AI to fake testimony in Minnesota case against conservative YouTuber
item.description: Jeff Hancock, an expert on AI and misinformation, has been accused of fabricating an expert declaration by citing a study that was allegedly made up by AI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The best cozy games during a stressful Thanksgiving season
item.description: For many, video games are loud, violent, and competitive. But for some they are the opposite: quiet, soft, and low-stakes. Welcome to the world of cozy gaming.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Democrats flee X for Bluesky amid Musk-Trump alliance
item.description: Bluesky, an alternative social media platform to X — formerly known as Twitter — has experienced a large surge in users as Democrats, including a number of prominent figures on the left, announced their departure from the social media network owned by Elon Musk in the wake of the presidential electi
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Bezos, Musk rivalry heats up ahead of Trump’s second term
item.description: A social media spat between Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos this week underscored a sharpening rivalry between two of the world’s richest men at a time when both are trying to grow their sprawling business empires in President-elect Trump’s second term.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: AWS and Anthropic ink deal to accelerate model development, enhance AI chips
item.description: The announcement that Amazon Web Services (AWS) will be Anthropic’s primary training partner confirms rumors of an even tighter partnership between the two companies. They announced Friday that Anthropic will use AWS Trainium processors to train and deploy its Claude family of models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: SpaceX will deliver its first lunar terrain vehicle to the Moon by 2029
item.description: Hot on the heels of another productive test flight, two new lunar customers have been added to the flight manifest for SpaceX’s Starship. Eagle landing Lunar Outpost said on Thursday that SpaceX will deliver its first Lunar Terrain Vehicle, Eagle, to the Moon by 2029.
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz, Fast Company

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 24 proposed features. The four most recent additions include warnings upon the use of memory-access methods in sun.misc.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Archaeologists discover 4,000-year-old canals used to fish by predecessors of ancient Maya
item.description: Using drones and Google Earth imagery, archaeologists have discovered a 4,000-year-old network of earthen canals in what’s now Belize.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: Investors allege Meta did not fully disclose the risks that Facebook users’ personal information would be misused by Cambridge Analytica.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Windows 11 will soon be available on Meta Quest 3 headsets
item.description: Meta Quest 3 and Quest 3S headset owners will soon gain access to the “full capabilities” of Windows 11 in mixed reality, Microsoft announced at its Ignite conference this week.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: From vision to value: A strategic approach to generative AI adoption
item.description: Generative AI has the potential to redefine productivity, create novel applications, and reinvent customer experience.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AWS prepares to command an army of AI agents
item.description: According to a recent report from SNS Insider, the global AI agents market was valued at $3.7 billion in 2023. The market is expected to grow to $103.6 billion by 2032, with a compound annual growth rate of 44.9% during the forecast period from 2024 to 2032.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nile unwraps NaaS security features for enterprise customers
item.description: Network-as-a-service startup Nile has added new zero-trust capabilities and tapped Microsoft and Palo Alto Networks to bolster its enterprise security services. Nile was founded by former Cisco CEO John Chambers and Pankaj Patel, Cisco’s former chief development officer.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Backup Software vs. Backup as a Service (BaaS): The Key Differences
item.description: As data volumes increase exponentially, the threat of data loss from cyberattacks, human error, or system failure has never been greater — which is why, in 2025, fortifying a data protection strategy has never made more sense.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Consumer watchdog to oversee digital wallets, payment apps
item.description: Large companies that offer digital wallets and payment apps will now be overseen by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB). The CFPB finalized a rule Thursday to bring companies handling more than 50 million transactions a year under its supervision.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: AI networking a focus of HPE’s Juniper deal as Justice Department concerns swirl
item.description: Amid reports of US regulatory concerns over HPE’s proposed buy of Juniper Networks, the CEOs of both companies talked about the future of the blended entity at this week’s HPE Discover event in Barcelona – and that future includes plenty of AI networking technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: AI-powered dog robot sniffs out invasive fire ants
item.description: Fueled by artificial intelligence, CyberDog the robotic dog is designed specifically locate fire ant nests, which are endangering ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast is spinning off into a new company many of the cable television networks that were once at the entertainment giant's heart as consumers increasingly swap out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety as Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How the U.S. Space Force can modernize at a smarter pace
item.description: Modernizing space programs while maintaining operations is one of the most challenging things, and rings true for the U.S. Space Force. The post How the U.S. Space Force can modernize at a smarter pace first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Azure AI Foundry tools up for changes in AI applications
item.description: The way we use artificial intelligence is changing. Chatbots aren’t going away. We’ll continue to use them to deliver basic, natural language, self-service applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: IBM deal brings AMD Instinct accelerators to IBM Cloud
item.description: IBM is expanding its AI accelerator options for enterprise users of its cloud service. AMD Instinct MI300X accelerators are now available as a service on IBM Cloud, the vendors announced.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Battle for Infowars: A Heated Auction and Conspiracy Theories Unfold
item.description: A federal judge is asked to disqualify The Onion's bid for Infowars, alleging fraud and collusion.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: T-Mobile hacked by Chinese cyber espionage in major attack on US telecoms
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about T-Mobile being hacked in broad cyberattack on global phone and internet companies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
